title: Operator Station (mediated human-attention loop)
summary: The builder passed the mediated-attention viability gate. Production Station contract node #114 is ready; application work still waits on that contract and the shared #12 application-client-ready checkpoint.

nodes.title: Vertical spike: mediated human-attention loop
nodes.summary: Build one deliberately narrow Windows experimental loop: a worker sends to a stable attention address; an operator agent filters and escalates to a desktop-attended address; the Station shows the message and accepts a reply; the operator agent routes the response back with source provenance intact. Temporary CLI/library integration is allowed and must be documented rather than treated as the production contract.

nodes.title: Mediated attention loop is worth productionizing
nodes.summary: Builder dogfoods the spike with several real sessions during a focused work period and decides whether it materially reduces terminal tab polling, preserves enough context, routes replies naturally, and filters at the right level. The gate may pass, reshape the boundary, or stop the workstream; no production contract work proceeds before it.

nodes.title: Production Station and operator-loop contract
nodes.summary: Using spike evidence, establish and document the accepted product boundary: human-attended address semantics; source/provenance conventions; namespaced message kinds and metadata; notification policy; reply/disposition behavior; direct, assisted, and quiet routing modes; operator-agent recovery expectations; identity and safety limits. Contribute application-client requirements to shared issue #12 rather than owning a desktop-only client contract.

nodes.title: Production Windows Operator Station
nodes.summary: Implement the separately installable Windows desktop Station under the accepted product contract and the campaign's shared #12 application-client-ready checkpoint: configured operator addresses, actionable feed and threads, notification policy, reply and disposition, direct/assisted operation, backend selection, provenance display, restart/backfill behavior, and safe links without arbitrary command execution. Sketch; detail at promotion after station-contract.

nodes.title: Reusable operator-agent role and routing policy
nodes.summary: Package and document a reusable operator-agent role that attends worker-facing ingress addresses, resolves or clarifies routine messages, aggregates and summarizes related traffic, escalates human obligations with source provenance and recommendations, receives human replies, routes outcomes back, and dispositions both raw and mediated messages. It remains a reference integrator rather than Telex core behavior. Sketch; detail at promotion after station-contract.

nodes.title: Production Station and operator loop are usable
nodes.summary: Builder validates the production app and operator role together through direct and assisted scenarios. The system must make source provenance legible, route replies predictably, avoid obvious notification overload, and remain optional to ordinary Telex use before hardening proceeds.

nodes.title: Postgres, recovery, notification, security, and packaging hardening
nodes.summary: Harden the accepted loop through realistic use: networked Postgres and remote senders; Station and operator-agent restart/offline recovery; unoccupied and stale addresses; noisy workloads and digest/suppression behavior; duplicate delivery; delayed replies; sender/provenance presentation; safe link handling; install/upgrade/auto-start expectations; documentation and troubleshooting. Produce a concise validation report for closure. Sketch; detail after usable-loop-gate.

nodes.title: Human-attention loop validated and workstream reconciled
nodes.summary: Builder runs the hardened Station with several real local and remote sessions, confirms direct and mediated operation, validates recovery and notification posture, reviews the operational-hardening evidence, and accepts the exported Station/operator-loop contract. Reconciliation records lessons for future Streamliner portfolio-attention work.

checkpoints.title: Mediated attention loop proven valuable
checkpoints.summary: A real vertical loop exists and builder dogfooding has established that it is worth productionizing.

checkpoints.title: Production Station and operator role usable
checkpoints.summary: The accepted contract, desktop Station, and reusable operator-agent role work together and have passed builder UX validation.

checkpoints.title: Operator Station operationally validated
checkpoints.summary: The human-attention loop has survived Postgres, restart/offline, notification-pressure, provenance, safety, and packaging validation and is ready for closure.
